summary: Matched eight small Kirby callback functions in ftKb_Init.c by replacing easy_func placeholders with simple one-call wrappers to existing fighter/Kirby helper routines. The decomp-dev bot reported +256 matched bytes and 8 new 100% function matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; converted eight Kirby placeholder functions into matching wrapper implementations.

what_changed: Implemented ftKb_SpecialLw1_Phys as a wrapper calling ft_80084FA8(gobj).
Implemented ftKb_SpecialLwEnd_Phys as a wrapper calling ft_80085134(gobj).
Implemented ftKb_SpecialAirLwStart_Phys as a wrapper calling ft_80085134(gobj).
Implemented ftKb_SpecialAirLwEnd_Phys as a wrapper calling ft_80085134(gobj).
Implemented ftKb_SpecialNCapture1_Anim as a wrapper calling ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8(gobj).
Implemented ftKb_LgSpecialN_Phys as a wrapper calling ft_80084F3C(gobj).
Implemented ftKb_FxSpecialAirNEnd_Coll as a wrapper calling ftCo_AirCatchHit_Coll(gobj).
Implemented ftKb_MsSpecialAirNEnd_Phys as a wrapper calling ft_80084EEC(gobj).
The matching report recorded eight new matches, each +32 bytes, for a total of +256 matched bytes and +0.01% matched code.
smart_moves: Grouped several extremely small callback placeholders into one low-risk PR instead of treating each 32-byte wrapper separately.
Reused already-known shared fighter physics/collision helpers rather than attempting to inline or over-describe behavior.
Recognized that multiple semantically distinct Kirby callbacks can legitimately dispatch to the same helper, such as ft_80085134 for several SpecialLw air/ground end/start physics callbacks.
Kept the implementations minimal: each function accepts the existing Fighter_GObj* argument and forwards it directly to the matching helper.
decomp_lessons: For remaining easy_func placeholders, check whether the entire function is just a one-argument forwarding wrapper to a common fighter helper.
Physics and collision callbacks in character init/source files often match as tiny wrappers around generic ft_80084xxx/ft_80085xxx or ftCo_* routines.
Do not infer complex move behavior when the assembly shape is consistent with a single direct helper call; a minimal wrapper is enough for matching.
Bot match reports are useful evidence for these micro-PRs: here every added wrapper moved from 0.00% to 100.00% and contributed exactly +32 bytes.
naming_conventions: Kirby functions use the ftKb_ prefix.
Callback suffixes indicate callback role: _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll.
Air variants are named with SpecialAir in the function name, such as ftKb_SpecialAirLwStart_Phys and ftKb_SpecialAirLwEnd_Phys.
Copied-character or ability-specific Kirby variants retain abbreviated prefixes in the middle of the name, such as LgSpecial, FxSpecial, LkSpecial, and MsSpecial.
Shared common fighter functions retain ft_800xxxxx names when not yet semantically named; common fighter collision helpers can use ftCo_* names, as with ftCo_AirCatchHit_Coll.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Look for 32-byte PowerPC wrapper functions that load/pass the gobj argument and branch/call one helper.
When several adjacent placeholders have identical callback signatures, compare their action-state callback table context to identify the correct generic helper.
A direct one-call C body of the form helper(gobj); was sufficient to match these functions.
Repeated helper targets are acceptable when different action-state callbacks share the same default physics behavior.

review_feedback.summary: GALE01 report showed matched code increased to 44.60% (+0.01%, +256 bytes) with 8 new 100% matches in ftKb_Init.
No human review comments or PR body rationale were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Search ftKb_Init.c for remaining /// #ftKb_*_Phys placeholders near SpecialLw and copied Kirby moves.
Search for other fighter easy_func placeholders that are likely one-call wrappers to ft_80084FA8, ft_80085134, ft_80084F3C, or ft_80084EEC.
Compare adjacent action-state callback functions for copied Kirby abilities where _Anim, _Phys, or _Coll callbacks may just forward to shared helper routines.
Search for ftCo_AirCatchHit_Coll wrappers in other characters or Kirby copied-move collision callbacks.
